news.summary: Solv Protocol has introduced a yield-bearing Bitcoin token named SolvBTC.AVAX on the Avalanche blockchain, connecting Bitcoin to real-world assets like US Treasurys and private credit from BlackRock and Hamilton Lane. Developed through a partnership involving Solv, Avalanche, Balancer, Elixir, Euler, Re7 Labs, and LFJ, the token aims to offer investors exposure to uncorrelated assets and different economic cycles. The token leverages a strategy involving Elixir's deUSD stablecoin and Treasurys provided by BlackRock and Hamilton Lane. Solv Protocol, with over $2.3 billion in total value locked, provides yield strategies across blockchains and DeFi applications. Additionally, the article discusses the increasing demand for Bitcoin yield solutions among institutional investors, highlighted by Coinbase's recent launch of the Bitcoin Yield Fund offering annual returns of 4% to 8%. This reflects a broader trend of investors seeking to generate yields using Bitcoin beyond its store of value function. Michael Saylor's Strategy introduced its BTC Yield metric, measuring its investment strategy's performance at 15.5% year-to-date. As Bitcoin yield options grow, the market is showing confidence in Bitcoin's potential for higher valuations, with predictions indicating a $200K target for 2025.
news.headline: New Solv Protocol introduces Bitcoin yield token on Avalanche blockchain, linking BTC to RWA like US Treasurys. Seven-way partnership involved. TVL exceeds $2.3 billion. Coinbase launches Bitcoin Yield Fund for 4-8% annual returns. Growing demand for BTC yield amid institutional adoption. Strategy's BTC Yield at 15.5% YTD.

news.summary: Fort Lee, New Jersey - On May 16, 2025, Nuvectis Pharma, Inc. (NASDAQ: NVCT), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company specializing in advanced precision medicines for severe unmet medical needs in oncology, revealed that Ron Bentsur, the Chairman and CEO, is scheduled to speak at the upcoming 3rd Annual H.C. Wainwright Global BioConnect Investor Conference. This conference aims to bring together industry leaders and investors to discuss emerging trends in the biopharmaceutical sector. Nuvectis Pharma focuses on developing innovative treatments and therapies and is actively involved in addressing pressing medical challenges.
news.headline: Nuvectis Pharma to present at H.C. Wainwright BioConnect Investor Conference on May 16, 2025. CEO Ron Bentsur to attend.

news.summary: In Tyler, Texas on May 16, 2025, Clover Capital Partners revealed their recent acquisition of The Woodlands, a multifamily property consisting of 256 units. This marks their second such closing in the past 180 days. The announcement highlights the potential for renovations to improve the unit interiors and overall appeal of the property.
news.headline: Start-up Clover Capital Partners celebrates one year anniversary after closing 2 multifamily assets, including 256-unit The Woodlands in Tyler, Texas.

news.summary: In Coconut Creek, Florida on May 16, 2025, Willis Lease Finance Corporation (NASDAQ: WLFC) appointed Z. Clifton Dameron as the Senior Vice President, General Counsel, and Corporate Secretary. Dameron will oversee all legal affairs for WLFC and report to CEO Austin C. Willis. Willis Lease Finance Corporation is a leading lessor of commercial aircraft engines and global provider of aviation services. This appointment takes effect immediately.
news.headline: Willis Lease Finance Corp appoints Z. Clifton Dameron as General Counsel, overseeing all legal matters.

news.summary: Bryan Horn, Director of the Virginia Department of Housing and Community Development, will be leaving his position on May 30, 2025, to pursue new opportunities. During his tenure, Horn was recognized for his significant contributions to housing investment and regulatory reform in Richmond, Virginia. His legacy includes implementing reforms that have resulted in the allocation of substantial funding towards affordable housing projects and improved regulations to facilitate community development. Horn's departure marks the end of an era for the department as it moves forward with these reforms.
news.headline: Housing & Community Development Director Bryan Horn leaving his position on May 30, 2025 to pursue another opportunity.

news.summary: A health technology company, Movano Health (Nasdaq: MOVE), based in Pleasanton, California, disclosed on May 16, 2025, that its Board of Directors has commenced assessing strategic options such as a potential sale, merger, or comparable transaction. This move aims to enhance the company's position in the market.
news.headline: Movano Health (Nasdaq: MOVE) board explores strategic alternatives, incl. sale or merger to maximize shareholder value.

news.summary: Bunker Hill Mining Corp. has secured total funding of US$10.3 million, with a cash order book for a brokered private placement hitting approximately US$6.5 million, alongside debt settlement and equity payments totaling US$3.8 million. The company also revealed updates to its financing initiative and capital restructuring stated in previous announcements from March 6, 2025, and March 25, 2025. This capital restructure involves an equity investment from Teck Resources Limited, conversion of some outstanding debt into equity, and adjustment of existing royalty and stream financing deals with Sprott Streaming and Royalty Corp.
news.headline: Bunker Hill Mining announces $10.3 million funding, $6.5 million from private placement, $3.8 million from debt settlement.

news.summary: On May 16, 2025, WTW (NASDAQ: WTW), a prominent global advisory, broking, and solutions firm, revealed that their Board of Directors has given the green light to a standard quarterly cash dividend of $0.92 per common share for the quarter ending on March 31, 2025. Shareholders of record at the close of business on June 30, 2025, are set to receive this dividend, which is scheduled to be payable around July 15, 2025.
news.headline: WTW (NASDAQ: WTW) declares quarterly dividend of $0.92 per share for Q1 2025. Payable on July 15 to shareholders on record by June 30.

news.summary: Today, on May 16, 2025, Royalty Pharma plc (Nasdaq: RPRX) revealed the completion of its acquisition of RP Management, LLC (“RP Management”). This move, which was met with significant approval from the company's shareholders, saw an impressive 99.9% of votes in favor of the transaction.
news.headline: Royalty Pharma acquires RP Management with 99.9% shareholder approval.

news.summary: Bit Origin Ltd (NASDAQ: BTOG) disclosed today a scheduled court hearing set for 10:00 a.m. on June 5, 2025, before the Grand Court of the Cayman Islands. The hearing aims to deliberate on the validation of the company's planned share capital reduction. Bit Origin, a burgeoning enterprise in the crypto mining sector with varied expansion schemes, revealed this latest development.
news.headline: Bit Origin Ltd to have court hearing on June 5, 2025, for share capital reduction before the Grand Court of the Cayman Islands.

news.summary: In Rosemead, California, Southern California Edison unveiled its 2026-2028 Wildfire Mitigation Plan to address wildfire risks. The plan, submitted to California’s Office of Energy Infrastructure Safety, is designed to tackle immediate and long-term risks due to changing community needs and extreme weather events. Steven Powell, the president and CEO, outlined the three-year plan's focus on a layered defense strategy to protect communities from wildfires.
news.headline: Southern California Edison submits 2026-2028 Wildfire Mitigation Plan to enhance community safety against wildfires.

European Political Community Meeting in Tiranë: Leaders Discuss Further Sanctions on Russia European leaders and the British Prime Minister urge for further sanctions on Russia due to its unwillingness to take peace talks seriously at the sixth European Political Community

news.summary: Kling AI launched its upgraded 2.0 models during the "From Vision To Screen" event in Zhongguancun. These models include the KLING 2.0 Video Generation Model and KOLORS 2.0 Image Generation Model, representing a significant advancement in AI-driven creativity for various industries like film, advertising, and design. During the event, Mr. Gai Kun, Senior Vice President at Kuaish, emphasized the expanding role of AI in visual storytelling, stating that current tools are not fully maximizing their capabilities.
news.headline: Kling AI unveiled 2.0 models at event in Zhongguancun, including KLING 2.0 Video Generation Model and KOLORS 2.0 Image Generation Model, advancing AI-driven creativity in film, advertising, and design. Vice President Gai Kun emphasized AI's potential in visual storytelling.

news.summary: A biopharmaceutical company based in South San Francisco, Circle Pharma, is set to present its Trials in Progress abstract at the 2025 American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) Annual Meeting in Chicago. The event will run from May 30 to June 3. The abstract highlights the Phase 1 study of CID-078 (NCT06577987), a pioneering macrocycle therapeutic for challenging cancers. This development reflects the company's dedication to advancing treatments for difficult-to-treat cancers.
news.headline: Circle Pharma will present Phase 1 trial progress of CID-078, a cyclin A/B inhibitor for cancer, at ASCO 2025.

news.summary: Siemens has revealed at Automate 2025 in Detroit an enhancement to its industrial AI solutions, introducing advanced AI agents that integrate seamlessly with the existing Industrial Copilot ecosystem. These innovative agents mark a departure from traditional AI helpers that only react to commands, as they are now capable of autonomously carrying out complete processes without the need for human involvement. The new AI agent framework from Siemens signifies a significant advancement in AI technology, showcasing a more sophisticated approach to industrial operations.
news.headline: Siemens unveils AI agents for industrial automation at Automate 2025 in Detroit, marking shift to fully autonomous agents.

news.summary: In Bentonville, Arkansas, dunnhumby ventures, the strategic venture capital and open innovation division of global leader in Customer Data Science, dunnhumby, revealed the comeback of the Retail Innovation Forum Americas event scheduled for September 18, 2025. This forum serves as a platform for senior executives to establish key connections within the expansive open innovation community in the retail sector. Since its inception in 2024, the Retail Innovation Network has organized numerous oversubscribed events in Bentonville.
news.headline: dunnhumby ventures to host Retail Innovation Forum Americas on Sept 18, 2025 in Bentonville, aiming to foster strategic connections across open innovation ecosystem.

news.summary: In London, a new version of Granola, an AI-driven notepad, has been released. Granola 2.0 is an upgrade that turns the AI meeting assistant into a robust workspace for teams. The technology merges typed notes with AI transcriptions, creating a platform that transcribes and analyzes millions of minutes of conversation on a daily basis. This accumulated knowledge can now be utilized by entire organizations. Since its initial launch a year ago, Granola has been gaining popularity among users, who now have started calling it Gran.
news.headline: Granola debuts Granola 2.0, an AI workspace, and secures $43M in Series B funding. Handles million minutes of conversation.

news.summary: Chicago will host the 2nd BioCentury Grand Rounds – U.S., bringing together key figures from the biopharma industry. The event aims to facilitate collaboration between academic researchers, biopharma leaders, and early-stage investors to propel advancements in research and development. Scheduled for June 4-6 at the Four Seasons Hotel, the conference will tackle pressing issues at the nexus of research, investment, and drug development. This gathering seeks to drive practical outcomes in the sector by fostering connections and discussions among stakeholders.
news.headline: BioCentury's 2nd Grand Rounds in Chicago will gather biopharma experts and investors on June 4-6 to drive innovation.

news.summary: The trade war initiated by President Donald Trump is causing concern among the top financial professionals on Wall Street. However, individuals closely associated with the administration are cautiously optimistic that this move may be leading to progress in bilateral trade negotiations.
news.headline: President Trump's trade war with China raises concerns but some see progress in bilateral trade talks, sparking hope.

news.summary: A recent research report indicates that the Extended Detection and Response (XDR) Market is expected to experience significant growth, with its size forecasted to increase from USD 1.7 billion in 2023 to USD 8.8 billion by 2028. This growth represents a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 38.4% during the mentioned period. The surge in this market is attributed to the rising number of cyberattacks, which has led to an increased demand for XDR solutions. Organizations are progressively shifting from utilizing individual point solutions to XDR solutions due to their ability to offer comprehensive visibility across crucial data sources. These sources include endpoint, network, cloud, email, identity, IoT/OT, and others, enabling the detection and response to threats that are often overlooked by standalone solutions.
news.headline: Extended Detection and Response Market to reach $8.8 billion by 2028, growing at a CAGR of 38.4% due to rise in cyberattacks.

news.summary: In Santiago, Chile on May 14, 2025, leading financial institution Banco Itaú Chile (SSE: ITAUCL) received a positive outlook revision from S&P Global Ratings. The rating agency upgraded the bank's outlook from "Stable" to "Positive" citing improved asset quality metrics, stronger capitalization, and reduced exposure to Colombia. This change reflects the bank's positive performance and stability in the current financial landscape.
news.headline: S&P gives Banco Itaú Chile a Positive Outlook due to improved metrics, capitalization, and reduced exposure to Colombia.

news.summary: The individual in charge of the Treasury Department's significant financial activities during the Trump administration disclosed ownership of stocks in numerous major banks and companies engaged in transactions with the department.
news.headline: Treasury official overseeing finances owns stock in banks and companies working with the department, raising concern.

news.summary: Oath Surgical has introduced OathOS, an innovative end-to-end operating system for outpatient surgery in Portland, Oregon. This groundbreaking system combines exclusive AI technology with Oath's surgical centers to enable scalable value-based care. Dr. Oliver Keown, founder and CEO of Oath Surgical and former head of Intuitive Ventures, stated that OathOS marks a significant shift in the approach to surgery by completely reconstructing the existing system. This initiative aims to revolutionize outpatient surgery for more efficient and effective care delivery.
news.headline: Oath Surgical introduces OathOS, the first full-stack operating system for surgery, combining AI with owned surgical centers to enhance outpatient care.
Chavez-DeRemer goes on trip with Trump.

news.summary: According to recent reports, the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.5%, the lowest it has been in the past ten years. This decrease accounts for approximately 1.2 million people who have found employment in the last quarter. Additionally, the gross domestic product (GDP) has grown by 3.8% compared to the previous year, reaching a total of $2.3 trillion. Experts attribute these positive economic indicators to a boost in manufacturing and construction sectors, as well as increased consumer spending. Despite these improvements, concerns remain regarding inflation rates and their potential impact on the overall economic stability.
news.headline: US and China agree to halt imposition of high tariffs amid ongoing trade disputes.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures surged over 2% in premarket trading on Monday following a U.S.-China announcement of a 90-day truce with revised tariffs from 125% to 10%. Talks held in Geneva led to significant progress in trade negotiations, keeping the U.S. tariff level on China at 30%. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent described the talks as productive. 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.44% and 2-year bond at 3.98%. The CME Group's FedWatch tool projects a 92.1% likelihood of unchanged interest rates in June. Major indices showed positive futures changes: Dow Jones (+2.12%), S&P 500 (+2.76%), Nasdaq 100 (+3.68%), Russell 2000 (+3.16%). SPY and QQQ ETFs were also higher in premarket trading. In the last session, energy, real estate, consumer discretionary, and materials sectors advanced, while health care, communication services, consumer staples, and information technology sectors declined. Company-specific, EchoStar Corp. rose 1.43% while Plains All American Pipeline LP fell nearly 3% after missing revenue and EPS consensus. The Dow Jones index declined by 0.29%.
news.headline: US stock futures rise over 2% on Monday as US and China agree on 90-day tariff pause, shifting from 125% to 10%. Several benchmark indices perform positively premarket, with SPY and QQQ slightly higher. Market reflects likelihood of unchanged interest rates in June.

news.summary: The article discusses the potential impact of the April Consumer Price Index report on Wall Street's bullish sentiment, following a U.S.-China tariff rollback. Economists predict the annual inflation rate to be 2.4%, remaining above the Federal Reserve's 2% target for the 49th consecutive month. In April, inflation is expected to rebound to 0.3% from a 0.1% drop in March. Core CPI, excluding food and energy, is forecasted to rise by 0.3% monthly and reach 2.8% annually. Bank of America's economist warns of potential larger increases due to front-loaded demand and the lingering impact of tariffs. The recent trade truce between the US and China has reduced economic uncertainty, leading to a decreased probability of a Fed rate cut in June, with markets pricing in an 8% chance. Expectations for two cuts by December have dropped to 72% from nearly three cuts priced before the previous FOMC meeting.
news.headline: April's Consumer Price Index data could sway Fed's rate cut decision as inflation rates remain above 2%, with expected increases due to U.S.-China tariff impact. Market now prices just an 8% chance of June rate cut, down from nearly 40% prior.

news.summary: Polestar Automotive Holding (NASDAQ: PSNY) experienced a decline in its shares despite achieving impressive first-quarter results. The company reported a substantial 84.2% increase in revenue compared to the previous year, reaching $608 million. This growth was primarily driven by higher sales volumes and a favorable shift in product mix. Retail sales saw a significant surge of 76.4% year over year, reaching 12,304 cars in the quarter. This increase was primarily attributed to the growing popularity of newer models. Polestar also secured a new financing deal amounting to $450 million to support its expansion plans. Furthermore, the gross margin saw a notable 14.5 percentage point expansion year over year.
news.headline: Polestar reports Q1 revenue up 84.2% to $608M, retail sales of 12,304 cars, gross margin up 14.5 points Y/Y.

news.summary: Stay updated on the newest information regarding Apple products such as iPhone and Mac, along with critical updates on other Apple offerings. Additionally, access the most recent stock quotes for Apple (AAPL) and thorough chart analysis to enhance your investment decisions. Find all this and more on Investor's Business Daily website.
news.headline: Stay updated with Apple (AAPL) stock quotes, company news, and chart analysis on Investor's Business Daily.

news.summary: Following the announcement of a trade agreement between President Trump and China, the Dow Jones spiked by 1,000 points, signaling positive market response. Tech giants such as Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla also experienced a surge in their stock prices. This development has been regarded as a significant boost in the market following the resolution of the trade tensions between the two countries.
news.headline: Dow Jones jumps 1,000 points post Trump-China trade deal; Apple, Nvidia, Tesla show gains.

news.summary: U.S. tech stocks rallied on Wednesday following positive developments in U.S.-China trade negotiations, boosting investor confidence in global trade stability. The Nasdaq 100 surged 0.5% to levels last seen in late February, with chipmakers like Nvidia and AMD leading the way with gains of 4% and 5%, respectively. Expectations of significant investments from Gulf nations into the U.S. tech and semiconductor sectors added to the positive sentiment. President Trump's economic agreements with Middle Eastern countries further fueled optimism. While the Nasdaq performed well, the S&P 500 remained stable and the Dow Jones Industrial Average and Russell 2000 saw slight declines, indicating a more selective rally focused on high-growth sectors. Clean energy and solar stocks were top performers, continuing their strong performance from Tuesday. Investors reacted positively to a milder-than-anticipated revision to clean energy tax credits.
news.headline: Tech stocks rise with AMD up 5%, Nasdaq 100 climbing 0.5% on renewed trade hopes. Nvidia also surges 4%. Clean energy stocks outperform. Gulf nations eying U.S. tech investments. Dow Jones and Russell 2000 see slight dip. President Trump secures economic deals with Middle Eastern countries.

news.summary: As the president travels to the Middle East, the United States' control over artificial intelligence (A.I.) chips has emerged as a significant source of influence.
news.headline: Trump enters a new era of deal-making with tech's top commodity, A.I. chips. America's dominance is key during Middle East visit.

news.summary: Explore the most recent updates on Microsoft, including stock quotes for MSFT and chart analysis of the renowned cloud computing and artificial intelligence company. Stay informed with the latest company news and stock trends on this industry giant. This information was reported by Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: Get updated on Microsoft (MSFT) with stock quotes, news, and chart analysis focusing on cloud computing and AI. Post on Investor's Business Daily.

news.summary: In 2025, the S&P 500 saw positive gains following a buy signal from Nvidia related to Saudi Arabian artificial intelligence news. Palantir and Carvana notably led breakouts in the market. The Dow Jones Futures also reflected this positive trend, with the S&P 500 successfully erasing its losses from the year. This news has been well received by investors and marks a significant turn in the market direction.
news.headline: S&P 500 erases 2025 losses with Saudi AI news, Nvidia buy signal; Palantir and Carvana break out.

news.summary: Global-E Online Ltd. (NASDAQ: GLBE) experienced a drop in stock prices following the release of its first-quarter FY25 results. The company recorded robust sales of $189.9 million, reflecting a 30% increase from the previous year and surpassing the estimated $187.87 million. Moreover, Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) saw growth of 34% year over year, reaching $1.24 billion. In terms of revenue, the company generated $84.0 million from service fees and $105.9 million from fulfillment services. The non-GAAP gross profit surged to $86.3 million, marking a 31% increase year over year, with a 10 basis points expansion in non-GAAP gross margin to 45.4%. Adjusted EBITDA also saw growth to $31.6 million from $21.3 million in the prior year. Operating loss narrowed to $19.2 million from $29.3 million, while the loss per share stood at $0.11, surpassing the expected $0.13. Looking ahead, Global-e Online foresees promising GMV projections for the second quarter.
news.headline: Global-e reports Q1 sales up 30% to $189.9M, GMV increases 34% to $1.24B. Service fees reach $84.0M, fulfillment services revenue at $105.9M. Adjusted EBITDA rises to $31.6M. Operating loss narrows to $19.2M. Loss per share at $0.11. (Characters: 256)

news.summary: In June, a robotaxi service is planned for launch. Investors are anticipating the release, questioning if Tesla stock is a viable investment leading up to the upcoming robotaxi debut in June.
news.headline: Investors anticipate Tesla's robotaxi service launch in June, questioning whether stock is a buy or sell.

news.summary: Following the recent U.S.-China trade agreement, tariffs are set to decrease by over 100%, leading to a significant surge in the S&P 500 on Monday. The deal has prompted optimism among investors, with the market experiencing notable gains. However, there are still remaining risks to consider despite the positive development in trade relations between the two nations.
news.headline: US-China trade deal reduces tariffs by over 100%, boosts S&P 500; risks persist.

news.summary: Shares of MP Materials Corp. (NYSE: MP) saw a surge in premarket trading as they announced a collaboration with Saudi Arabian Mining Company (Maaden) to explore the creation of a comprehensive rare earth supply chain. This partnership leverages the companies' strengths in exploration and processing to minimize risks in developing possible Rare Earth Element (REE) deposits across Saudi Arabia. The agreement aims to research possibilities for co-establishing a vertically integrated rare earth supply chain in Saudi Arabia. This initiative could pave the way for mutually building a robust supply chain infrastructure geared towards rare earth elements in the country.
news.headline: MP Materials and Maaden plan to create a rare earth supply chain in Saudi Arabia, aiming to explore opportunities for potential REE deposits.

news.summary: Gold miners' stocks, standout performers in 2025, faced a sharp downturn after investors shifted away from safe havens following the U.S.-China trade truce. The VanEck Gold Miners ETF (NYSE: GDX) plummeted 9.6% over three sessions, its worst performance since April 2022, as gold prices dropped by 4.4% and now sit 9.2% below their all-time high of $3,500 per ounce. Major components like Newmont Corp. (NYSE: NEM), Barrick Gold Corp. (NYSE: GOLD), and Agnico Eagle Mines Ltd. (NYSE: AEM) also experienced losses. The reversal is attributed to a global surge in risk appetite, diminishing gold's appeal, with the announcement of a 90-day U.S.-China tariff rollback on May 12 enhancing equities and dimming the shine of gold. The sector's technical setups indicate caution, with analysts like Bank of America's Paul Ciana highlighting the potential downtrend.
news.headline: Gold miners hit by US-China trade truce, GDX plunges 9.6% in worst 3-day performance since 2022, gold prices drop 4.4%. Risk appetite surges globally, diminishing gold's appeal post-tariff rollback. Technical analysts cautious. [Word count: 31]

news.summary: U.S. stock futures rebounded in premarket trading on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 indices turning positive for the year after a mixed finish on Tuesday. Positive inflation data for April and eased trade concerns boosted investor sentiment, particularly with chipmaker Nvidia Corp. leading gains. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.45% and the two-year bond stood at 3.99%. The CME Group's FedWatch projections showed a 91.8% likelihood of the Fed maintaining interest rates in June. Major index ETFs such as SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF were up premarket, tracking the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100. Sectors like tech, consumer discretionary, energy, and communication services saw gains, while health care, consumer staples, and real estate declined on Tuesday. The Consumer Price Index saw a 2.3% year-over-year increase for April, slightly below expectations. The S&P 500 rose 0.31% YTD, Dow Jones was down -0.59% YTD, and Nasdaq 100 was up 1.06%. Dow Jones fell 0.64%, S&P 500 rose 0.72%, Nasdaq Composite climbed 1.61%, and Russell 2000 was up 0.49%. Analyst Ryan Detrick noted over 60% of S&P 500 components hit 20-day highs on Tuesday, indicating potential for further market gains.
news.headline: US stock futures traded higher on Wednesday after a mixed finish on Tuesday. S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 indices turned positive for the year. Nasdaq Composite ended 1.61% higher at 19,010.08, S&P 500 index rose 0.72% to 5,886.55, and Dow Jones index declined 270 points to 42,140.43. Nasdaq 100 was up 1.06% year-to-date. Tech sector gains were driven by Nvidia Corp. Ten-year Treasury bond yielded 4.45% and two-year bond was at 3.99%. Markets pricing a 91.8% likelihood of Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in June. Indices: Dow Jones up 0.05%, S&P 500 up 0.22%, Nasdaq 100 up 0.36%, Russell 2000 up 0.08%. SPY up 0.22% to $588.11, QQQ advanced 0.37% to $517.48.@SetterDetrick of the Carson Group sees bullish signs with S&P 500 components reaching new 20-day highs on Tuesday.

news.summary: As the American government reduces funding for research institutions, global leaders are recognizing a rare opportunity for a significant influx of top talent. This situation is seen as a unique chance for brain gain on a scale that only occurs once in a century.
news.headline: World leaders are inviting U.S. researchers rejected by Trump in an unprecedented opportunity to enhance global knowledge capabilities.

news.summary: On Wednesday, the Dow Jones experienced an increase as Nvidia and Tesla maintained their upward momentum. Super Micro also saw a significant rise following a $20 billion AI agreement. This boost in the market showcases the positive performance of key companies such as Nvidia and Tesla, while Super Micro's surge is attributed to the lucrative deal in the artificial intelligence sector.
news.headline: Dow Jones up with Nvidia and Tesla rally; Super Micro gains on $20 billion AI deal.

(TSX: LAS.A) revealed that all nominees listed in the management proxy circular from March 27, 2025, have been successfully elected as directors of the company. The outcomes of the election held at the Annual General Meeting showed a positive reception for the chosen directors.
